title: CTAB DNA Extraction for High Quality Molecular Weight DNA
author: Yaowu Yuan
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.3rsgm6e

steps.title: Grind plant tissue in a mortar cooled with liquid nitrogen
steps.instruction: Grind plant tissue in a mortar cooled with liquid nitrogen.

steps.title: Add 750 µL, CTAB DNA Extraction buffer
steps.instruction: Add 750 µL, CTAB DNA Extraction buffer .

steps.title: Wait until it warms up and...
steps.instruction: Wait until it warms up and becomes a green paste, then transfer to an eppie tube.

steps.title: Incubate the CTAB/plant extract mixture for...
steps.instruction: Incubate the CTAB/plant extract mixture for 00:15:00 at 55 °C in the heat block and invert to mix throughout the 15 minutes.

steps.title: Add 500 µL, Chloroform
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL, Chloroform : IsoAmyl Alcohol (24:1) in the hood and mix the solution by inverting the tubes ( do not vortex ).

steps.title: Centrifuge at for 00:10:00
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at for 00:10:00 .

steps.title: Transfer the upper aqueous phase only...
steps.instruction: Transfer the upper aqueous phase only to a new eppie tube (~ 500 µL ).

steps.title: Add RNase A (10 µg/ml)
steps.instruction: Add RNase A (10 µg/ml). 5 µL of 1mg/ml stock if you have 500 µL of sample.

steps.title: Incubate at 37 °C for 00:30:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37 °C for 00:30:00 .

steps.title: Add 50 µL, 7
steps.instruction: Add 50 µL, 7.5M Ammonium acetate followed by 500 µL, ice cold 100% ethanol and invert to mix.

steps.title: Put tubes in -20 °C, freezer...
steps.instruction: Put tubes in -20 °C, freezer for 1 hour (or longer) to precipitate the DNA.

steps.title: Centrifuge at for 00:15:00
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at for 00:15:00 . You should see a pellet at the bottom (align the tubes so that you know where the pellet is in case you can’t see it very well).

steps.title: Remove the supernatant and wash the DNA pellet as follows
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant and wash the DNA pellet as follows. (1/2)

steps.title: Add 500 µL, ice cold 70% ethanol
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL, ice cold 70% ethanol . (1/2)

steps.title: Centrifuge at for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at for 00:05:00 . (1/2)

steps.title: Remove the supernatant and wash the DNA pellet as follows
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant and wash the DNA pellet as follows. (2/2)

steps.title: Add 500 µL, ice cold 70% ethanol
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL, ice cold 70% ethanol . (2/2)

steps.title: Centrifuge at for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at for 00:05:00 . (2/2)

steps.title: Remove all the supernatant and allow...
steps.instruction: Remove all the supernatant and allow the DNA pellet to dry in the hood (approx. 00:20:00 ). Do not over dry the pellet since it will be hard to re-dissolve.

steps.title: Resuspend the DNA in 50 µL, dH2O
steps.instruction: Resuspend the DNA in 50 µL, dH2O .

steps.title: NanoDrop the sample to estimate the concentration
steps.instruction: NanoDrop the sample to estimate the concentration. Alternatively the DNA can be run on a gel to estimate the concentration or size of the DNA. Running a 0.4% gel overnight with the lambda DNA mono-cut ladder can give you an estimate of the size (it still doesn’t separate the large bands very well).
